milestoneTitle: Phase M9.12 — Dashboard panels scroll-center activation + tile contrast lift. The recessed .continue-row / .panel-tile recipe no longer fakes a hole through to the page bg; baseline tile background now sits ~55% of the way toward pure white so text contrast is stronger at rest. A new bidirectional IntersectionObserver on every .dash-panel with a narrow center band (rootMargin -40% 0 -40% 0, ~20% of viewport height) toggles an .is-activated class as the panel scrolls through the band. When activated, the panel's box-shadow deepens (raised elevation) and each child tile's bg reaches peak-white — both in a single 0.22s / 0.18s box-shadow + background transition, no transform and no sequenced chain, so the activation stays inside the newly-recorded 'animations sequence, never stack' rule and the 'no flicker on resize/repaint' guard. Only compositor-safe properties touched. New animation-sequencing / transform-exclusivity / no-flicker rules also recorded in CLAUDE.md design-language for future activations that layer shimmer / glow / transform.
